subject: Human Resources
title: The Wagner Act of 1935
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Wagner Act of 1935.

hook.text: In 1935, the U.S. passed a law that changed the balance of power between employers and workers forever.

buildup.text: The Wagner Act guaranteed workers the right to form unions, bargain collectively, and strike.

discovery.text: It created the National Labor Relations Board to enforce these rights and prevent unfair labor practices.

twist.text: Union membership exploded from 3 million to 15 million in a decade—reshaping the American workplace.

climax.text: The Wagner Act forced companies to negotiate with workers as equals—creating the field of labor relations.

punchline.text: One law gave millions of workers a voice.

quiz.question: What did the Wagner Act of 1935 guarantee?
options: Workers' right to form unions and bargain collectively
